task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenCAD login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD login screen is visible in a web browser, showing fields for email and password.

task_completion.description: Enter the Administrator email into the login form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'admin@opencad.local' is clearly visible in the email input field on the login screen.

task_completion.description: Successfully log into the OpenCAD system as the Administrator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD dashboard, portal, or MDT/CAD interface is visible, with UI elements indicating a successful login (e.g., 'Admin' or 'Administrator' in the user profile area).

task_completion.description: Select the Fire Department for the active session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Fire' is visible in the department selection dropdown, active unit display, or header, indicating it is the currently selected department.
An indicator showing the current status as '10-8' or 'Available' is clearly visible in the active interface for the logged-in user.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenCAD web interface using a browser, rather than using terminal commands or API scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser window (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) is visible displaying the OpenCAD interface, not a terminal window executing curl or database commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenCAD application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application appears to be a fully rendered web application with proper styling, layout, and interactive elements, not a static text file or mock HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the status or department.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Inspect Element) pane is not open and modifying DOM text to falsely display 'Fire' or '10-8'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Administrator account corresponds to user ID 2. The Fire Department corresponds to department ID 6 and is named 'Fire'. The standard APCO emergency services radio code for 'Available' or 'In Service' is '10-8'. Look for visual confirmation in the OpenCAD interface that the user is logged in as Admin, operating under the Fire department, and has an active status of 10-8 (Available).
